commit.message:
Add ML/DS algorithms from scratch: Parts 0-2

Part 0: Setup & utilities (helpers, metrics, synthetic data generators)
Part 1: Regression (8 algorithms — linear, polynomial, ridge, lasso, elastic net, Bayesian, LOESS)
Part 2: Classification (10 algorithms — logistic, KNN, naive Bayes, decision tree, SVM, perceptron, LDA)

Each algorithm includes theory, LaTeX math derivation, use cases, from-scratch implementation, and demo with visualizations. Only numpy + matplotlib, no external ML libraries.

commit.message:
Add skip_if_running flag to prevent overlapping cron sessions (#174)

## Summary
- Adds a per-schedule `skip_if_running` boolean flag (default `true`)
that prevents the scheduler from firing a new session when a previous
one from the same schedule is still active (`starting` or `running`)
- When a trigger is skipped, `next_run_at` is still advanced so the
schedule doesn't re-check the same due time
- Full-stack: DB schema + migration, scheduler logic, CRUD API,
TypeScript types, and frontend checkbox in the create/edit dialog

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify `python -m pytest gui/tests/` passes (125 tests)
- [ ] Create a schedule with `skip_if_running` enabled (default),
trigger it, confirm a second trigger while the first runs is skipped
(check logs for "skipped: session already running")
- [ ] Create a schedule with `skip_if_running` disabled, confirm
overlapping sessions are allowed
- [ ] Edit an existing schedule to toggle the flag, confirm it persists
- [ ] Open create/edit dialog, verify the checkbox appears below Role
and defaults to checked

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Home dashboard inflated task count (#40)

* Fix Home dashboard task counter inflating due to notes section checkboxes

Dataview's `page.file.tasks` was counting every checkbox in the daily note,
including `- [ ]` items embedded in `## Notes` content (e.g. checklists,
clarification bullets). Filter tasks by `section?.subpath === "Tasks"` so
only real work tasks under the `## Tasks` section are counted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix task count using line-range instead of section subpath

`t.section?.subpath` resolves to the immediate heading ("IS2", not "Tasks"),
so all tasks under project sub-headings were excluded. Instead, look up the
line numbers of `## Tasks` and `## Notes` via MetadataCache and only count
tasks whose line falls between those two boundaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Guard against daily notes missing ## Tasks heading

If a daily note has no ## Tasks heading, tasksLine stays -1 and
t.line > -1 would match every task, reverting the original bug.
Introduce an inTasksSection predicate that returns false when
## Tasks is not found, so the count shows 0 instead.

Also improve the comment to document the line-range approach and
the ## Notes boundary assumption.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add regression test for SoS Total invariant (must be between Played and Left)

Creates a scenario where the old set-union code would produce Total < min(Played, Left):
strong common opponents B and B2 shared between played and remaining, plus weak
played-only opponent C and moderate remaining-only opponents D/E/F. The weighted-average
fix produces Total ≈ 0.688, correctly between Played ≈ 0.667 and Left = 0.700.

commit.message:
fix: enable Keycloak SSO across all platform services

The platform was configured to suppress SSO at three layers:
1. Keycloak: custom browser-prompt-login flow disabled the Cookie
   authenticator, preventing session reuse across services
2. OAuth2-proxy (8 instances): --prompt=login forced re-authentication
   on every redirect regardless of Keycloak session state
3. Grafana: ?prompt=login appended to OIDC auth URL

Restore the default Keycloak browser flow (honors 30-minute SSO
session), remove --prompt=login from all oauth2-proxy deployments,
and strip the prompt parameter from Grafana's auth URL. Users now
log in once and get silent re-auth across all services until the
SSO session expires.

commit.message:
Elevate novel prediction to claim, document self-similar evolution

- New claim: floor-raising → lower discount rates → cooperative
  equilibria become sustainable. This is the cross-domain prediction
  that no component theory makes alone — the framework's test case
  for being a theory rather than a vocabulary.

- New analysis: the framework evolves through its own mechanism.
  Push → critique → incorporate → push. The development process
  is an instance of the pattern it describes. Documented honestly
  as observation, not proof.

commit.message:
feat: generate dedicated SSH key pair and disable password auth

- Auto-generate Ed25519 SSH key pair in ~/.ggo/ssh/ for TF studio containers
- Remove password authentication (PasswordAuthentication no, PermitRootLogin prohibit-password)
- Delete default password setup (removed: echo "root:ggo-studio" | chpasswd)
- Add IdentityFile to SSH config for seamless key-based authentication
- Display private key path in studio creation output
- Require SSH key for container access (fail if key generation fails)
- Improve security by enforcing key-only authentication

Key security improvements:
- Dedicated key pair for studio containers (not mixing with user's personal SSH keys)
- No password authentication reduces attack surface
- Auto-generated on first use, stored securely with 0600 permissions

commit.message:
fix: codesign worker signature mismatch, websocket arg order, model text visibility

CoDesign worker had 3 bugs preventing it from working:
1. stream_codesign_response() called with wrong kwargs (workflow_data/
   user_message vs actual user_request/current_workflow)
2. async generator called with sync for loop — wrapped with asyncio.run()
3. send_to_connection(endpoint, conn_id, payload) had wrong arg order,
   causing "unhashable type: dict" — fixed to (conn_id, payload, endpoint)

Also improved AIModelNode model context section visibility:
bg-muted/40 + text-muted-foreground was invisible on blue gradient,
changed to bg-white/10 + text-white/70.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: agent hang — add abort timeout, maxTurns, fix SDK message extraction

The agent subprocess was hanging indefinitely because:
1. No abort controller or timeout (now 120s)
2. No maxTurns limit (now 20)
3. Message extraction checked wrong shape (sdkMessage.content vs
   sdkMessage.message for assistant, sdkMessage.result for final answer)

commit.message:
Add discount rate and contemplative convergence analyses

Two new pieces pushing the framework's strongest threads:

- Discount rates: floor-raising lowers temporal discounting, which
  expands sustainable cooperative equilibria via the Folk Theorem.
  This is the framework's most novel formal contribution.

- Contemplative convergence: 5 independent traditions across 25
  centuries arriving at the same structural diagnosis. Honest about
  the 30-40% the framework hasn't reached (self-generated collapse,
  ethics-first, the apophatic dimension, death as teacher).

Incorporate multi-agent critique: fix overclaims, add honesty

Based on six-agent adversarial evaluation (adversarial, mathematical,
empirical, neuroscience, contemplative, game theory):

- "Not a metaphor" → "structural analogy" (honest about what it is)
- "Amygdala runs the show" → graded degradation (accurate neuroscience)
- "No biological ceiling" → distinguish tool-augmented from intrinsic
- "Interference, not incapacity" → acknowledge architectural limits
- "Convergence is evidence" → add epistemic caveats (shared training data)
- Add productive collapse section (when reduction is functional)
- Add scarcity vs maximization distinction (the causal variable question)
- Add threshold problem acknowledgment (defining "enough" is the contest)

commit.message:
Update dev-physics-lesson skill to require forge_shapes.h (#229)

* Update dev-physics-lesson skill to require forge_shapes.h

Physics lessons must now use common/shapes/forge_shapes.h for all scene
geometry instead of writing inline generation functions. If a lesson
needs a shape that the library does not provide, the shape must be added
to forge_shapes.h first.

Changes:
- Smart behavior: added bullet requiring forge_shapes.h for geometry
- Step 1 (Analyze): added check for missing shapes in forge_shapes.h
- Step 4 (main.c): replaced inline generate_sphere() example with
  forge_shapes.h API calls and the #include directive
- Conventions "Simple geometry": rewrote as forge_shapes.h call table
- Rendering baseline table: added procedural geometry row

Add vertex_count == 0 checks after forge_shapes_sphere() and
forge_shapes_cube() calls in the code example, matching the defensive
pattern used by the library itself.
